subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: CAPR −6.0% | VERA SL closed today, AdCom July 29 in 8 days
html_body:
Daily Portfolio Check

Friday, July 17, 2026 — Kuwait (UTC+3) · portfolio.json updated 2026-07-17

Open Position P&L

−$19.68 (−6.04%)

Today (est.)

−$3.52 (−1.1%)

VERA SL hit today (−$33.28 realized, done). CAPR −6% on broad chip/tech sell-off — thesis unchanged. AdCom July 29 in 8 trading days. Read FDA briefing docs when they drop (~Jul 24–27).

⚠️ Action required — week of July 20

FDA briefing documents expected ~July 24 (Fri) or July 27 (Mon). Read the FDA reviewer's language on the 2.4% LVEF endpoint. Skeptical tone → cut CAPR before July 29 open. Supportive tone → hold, GTC sell at $33–35 handles exit day-of.

Position Snapshot

💡 CAPR passes AdCom at $35 → 16×$35 = $560 + $450 cash = $1,010 ✓ goal hit

Position Detail

CAPR⚠️ WATCH$19.12 (−6.04% vs entry)

Down 6% from the $20.35 entry in 2 trading days, consistent with broad market weakness — chip/semi names led Nasdaq lower today (S&P −0.5%). No CAPR-specific negative news. HOPE-3 Phase 3 data unchanged, FDA lifted prior CRL with no new review issues flagged. The 2.4% LVEF endpoint is the open question — FDA briefing docs (expected Jul 24–27) are the real signal. Until then, thesis intact. Stop at $13 is 32% below current price — not threatened.

Stop: $13.00TP1: $35.00 — sell 100% AdCom dayAdCom: Jul 29 (Wed)
Trade math: Pass AdCom (60%) → 16×$35 − $326 cost − $1 comm = +$233 net (+71%)
Fail AdCom (40%) → ~$13 floor − $326 cost − $1 comm = −$119 net (−37%)
Expected value: 0.60×$233 − 0.40×$119 = +$92 EV (+28%) on $326 invested

VERA — Closed today · SL triggered @ $37.74

8 shares · blended entry $41.7755 · Net P&L: −$33.28 (−9.9%). Post-approval drift — TRUTAKNA FDA-approved July 7 (IgAN) but stock slid below $38 support. Cash proceeds $300.92 added to account. Done, no further action.

What to do this weekend & next week

Weekend (Jul 18–19): No action. GTC sell $33–35 is set in IBKR — nothing to do.
Mon–Fri (Jul 20–25): Watch fda.gov CTGTAC page for briefing docs. Set a Google Alert for "deramiocel briefing documents." Focus on FDA reviewer's language around the 2.4% LVEF endpoint specifically.
If skeptical → cut CAPR before July 29 open.
If supportive → do nothing. GTC handles exit day-of.

📚 Today you learned

PEG Ratio — and why it breaks for CAPR

PEG (Price/Earnings-to-Growth) = forward P/E ÷ earnings growth rate. Peter Lynch's shorthand for a cheap grower: PEG below 1.0 means you're paying less per unit of growth than that growth is worth. Works cleanly for profitable companies — NVDA at 40x forward growing 50% = PEG 0.8. But CAPR has no earnings. The denominator is zero, the ratio is undefined, and the entire traditional valuation framework collapses. The correct frame for a binary catalyst trade is probability-weighted expected return: 60% × +$233 win − 40% × $119 loss = +$92 expected value on $326 invested (+28%). That's the biotech equivalent of PEG < 1 — the math favors the trade at the assessed probability. The P&L tracker shows −6% today, but the expected value hasn't moved. Market noise is not thesis erosion.

Also today: Binary event premium — the extra implied volatility priced into CAPR options right now because a single AdCom vote can gap the stock 40–80% either direction. Why CAPR options are expensive pre-July 29.

Research output, not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results. Verify halal compliance quarterly on Zoya or Musaffa.
